text.title: AWS S3 Pre-Deployment Checklist
text.summary: Security defaults, data management, access patterns, and cost optimization checklists for S3 buckets.

rule.statement: Verify on every new bucket: Block Public Access enabled (4 settings), Object Ownership = BucketOwnerEnforced, default encryption configured (SSE-KMS or SSE-S3), bucket policy with aws:SecureTransport = false deny rule.
rule.rationale: These four settings are the minimum security baseline for any S3 bucket. Missing any one creates a vulnerability: public access, ACL-based data leaks, unencrypted objects, or plaintext HTTP access.

rule.statement: For production buckets: enable versioning, enable S3 access logging to a separate audit bucket, enable CloudTrail data events, verify SSE-KMS with customer-managed key and S3 Bucket Keys enabled.
rule.rationale: Versioning enables point-in-time recovery. Access logging and CloudTrail data events are required for compliance audits (SOC2, PCI) to prove access to specific objects at specific times.

rule.statement: Configure at minimum: abort incomplete multipart uploads after 7 days, transition non-current versions to Standard-IA after 30 days, expire non-current versions after 90 days, delete expired object delete markers.
rule.rationale: These four lifecycle rules prevent unbounded bucket growth from three common accumulation patterns: aborted uploads, old versions, and orphaned delete markers.

rule.statement: For SSE-C: verify it is explicitly enabled via PutBucketEncryption after April 2026 for any buckets that require customer-provided keys; audit existing SSE-C buckets before the deadline.
rule.rationale: AWS disabled SSE-C by default for new buckets starting April 2026. Teams migrating or creating new buckets requiring SSE-C must explicitly opt in — silent failure otherwise.

rule.statement: For CRR: confirm versioning on source and destination, IAM replication role with s3:ReplicateObject and s3:ReplicateDelete, destination bucket policy allowing the source role, KMS key sharing if encrypted.
rule.rationale: CRR silently fails if any prerequisite is missing — there is no blocking error at configuration time. Replication metrics and CloudWatch alarms on replication failures are required to detect misconfiguration.

antipattern.description: S3 buckets in production without any lifecycle rules configured.
antipattern.reason: Unmanaged buckets accumulate costs from three sources: incomplete multipart uploads, non-current versions (if versioned), and expired delete markers. A single active application can generate hundreds of GB of orphaned data per month.
